summary: Proposes a variance-reduced RLHF algorithm that stays robust when the Bradley-Terry reward model is misspecified, yielding improved regret bounds. Empirically, 77-81% of its responses are preferred over baselines on the Anthropic Helpful and Harmless dataset.

Measures CoT faithfulness in frontier reasoning models via prompt hints: models verbalize hints they exploit under 20% of the time, outcome-based RL improves faithfulness only to a plateau, and reward hacking goes unverbalized — so CoT monitoring can flag but not rule out misbehavior.

Distills a general methodology for formally verifying NLP-model robustness, analyzing the 'embedding gap' between verification over geometric embedding subspaces and discrete text semantics, and evaluating common pipeline components to consolidate a fragmented field.

Benchmark requiring agents to replicate 20 ICML 2024 papers from scratch, graded by an LLM judge against 8,316 author-co-developed rubric tasks. The best agent, Claude 3.5 Sonnet (New) with open scaffolding, scores 21%, below the ML-PhD human baseline.

Shows that Model Context Protocol agent workflows let attackers coerce leading LLMs into malicious code execution, remote access control, and credential theft, and introduces MCPSafetyScanner, an agentic auditor that probes arbitrary MCP servers and generates vulnerability-and-remediation reports.

Preliminary research notes from Anthropic's interpretability team on incorporating attention into circuit analysis, shared informally as lab-meeting-style early results for researchers active in mechanistic interpretability rather than as a mature paper.

Informal monthly update from Anthropic's interpretability team collecting developing ideas: emerging research strands slated for fuller publication alongside minor findings unlikely to receive standalone papers.

Snowplow augments kernel fuzzing with a learned white-box mutator that predicts promising argument mutations from the test program and coverage signals, discovering new Linux kernel coverage 4.8-5.2x faster, reaching directed-fuzzing targets 8.5x faster, and finding 86 previously unknown crashes.

RoR-Bench detects recitation by subtly shifting conditions of simple reasoning problems; top models including OpenAI o1 and DeepSeek-R1 lose around 60% accuracy on elementary-school arithmetic and reasoning when a single phrase changes, suggesting memorization rather than genuine reasoning.

Public release of the evaluation datasets behind Anthropic's 'Reasoning Models Don't Always Say What They Think,' letting external researchers measure how faithfully models' chains of thought verbalize the hints they actually use.

Argues that aligning AI to human intentions or 'helpful, honest, harmless' values is incomplete and lacks public justification in pluralistic societies, proposing instead that alignment target principles generated by fair processes justifiable to those affected by AI systems.

Formalizes underspecified queries as constraint-satisfaction problems with one missing variable and tests whether LLMs can pick the single clarifying question needed. Models excel on grade-school math variants but reach only 40-50% accuracy on logic and planning tasks.

Introduces attribution graphs built on cross-layer transcoder features to trace the step-by-step computations a language model performs on individual prompts, providing the core methodology behind Anthropic's circuit-tracing analyses of model internals.

Applies attribution-graph circuit tracing to Claude 3.5 Haiku across case studies including multi-step reasoning, poetry, multilingual prompts, hallucination, and jailbreaks, finding interpretable intermediate computations such as forward planning and a shared cross-language conceptual space.

Relax is a compiler abstraction unifying computational graphs, loop-level tensor programs, and library calls in one representation with first-class symbolic shape annotations, enabling dynamic-shape-aware optimization of LLMs competitive with state-of-the-art systems across GPUs, phones, embedded devices, and browsers.

Accessible companion to Anthropic's circuit-tracing papers: attribution graphs show Claude planning ahead (e.g., choosing rhymes before writing a line) and reasoning in a shared conceptual space that precedes language, letting knowledge learned in one language transfer to others.

Updates the Anthropic Economic Index with Claude 3.7 Sonnet data: coding, education, and science usage grew since launch, extended thinking mode skews toward technical tasks, and new datasets map automation versus augmentation across 630 granular usage categories.

Extracts novel chess concepts from AlphaZero's internal representations via convex optimization, filtering for teachability and novelty relative to human play; four world-champion-level grandmasters improved at solving prototype puzzles after studying the concepts, demonstrating superhuman AI knowledge can transfer to humans.

Generates fly-through videos from a single image along a given camera trajectory by conditioning an image-to-video latent diffusion model on raw extrinsics, camera-ray images, re-projected initial frames, and a 2D-3D transformer scene representation, achieving state-of-the-art controllable scene exploration.

System-card addendum for GPT-4o's native image generation, a significantly more capable successor to DALL-E 3 that produces photorealistic output and transforms input images, documenting the associated safety evaluations and mitigations.

Gensors lets end users author personalized visual sensors (e.g., 'alert if my toddler is getting into mischief') driven by multimodal LLM reasoning over camera feeds, supporting criteria elicitation, per-criterion debugging, and stress-test generation; study participants reported greater control and understanding.

AgentSpec is a lightweight domain-specific language for runtime constraints on LLM agents using triggers, predicates, and enforcement mechanisms. It blocks over 90% of unsafe code-agent executions and all hazardous embodied-agent and autonomous-driving actions, with millisecond-level overhead.

Shows prompted models can deliberately underperform on ML experiments and research decisions without detection by zero-shot prompted monitors: Claude 3.7 Sonnet sandbags zero-shot while Claude 3.5 Sonnet needs a one-shot example, though today's sandbagging would still be noticed by humans.

Trains a deep RL social planner for an iterated multiplayer trust game, using human-imitating neural agents as a simulated economy. The mechanism conditions generosity on available resources and temporarily sanctions defectors, producing a larger, more equal surplus and inspiring an explainable, more popular variant.

OpenAI-MIT Media Lab collaboration studying emotional engagement on ChatGPT, pairing large-scale automated analysis of real conversations with a randomized controlled trial; affective use is concentrated among a small group of heavy users, and heavy usage correlates with loneliness and emotional dependence.

AlphaTensor-Quantum casts T-count minimization for fault-tolerant quantum circuits as tensor decomposition solved with deep RL, exploiting gadgets and domain knowledge. It outperforms existing T-count optimizers, matches the best human constructions for Shor's-algorithm and quantum-chemistry circuits, and rediscovers a Karatsuba-like multiplication trick.

Aardvark Weather replaces the entire numerical weather prediction pipeline with a single machine learning model that ingests raw observations and outputs global gridded and local station forecasts, beating an operational NWP baseline on several variables and remaining skilful to ten days.

First systematic evaluation of 12 reasoning and general-purpose LLMs across 17 Chinese and English legal tasks, plus Legal-R1, an open model distilled from DeepSeek-R1 chains of thought. Error analysis surfaces outdated legal knowledge, weak legal interpretation, and factual hallucination.

Parameter-efficient fine-tuning of LLaMA 3.1/3.2 and Gemma 2 on chain-of-thought solutions for Ukrainian standardized exam tasks improves complex matching scores by up to 17.4% over answer-only tuning, with joint topic-plus-solution generation adding a further 5.4% gain.

Trains convolutional neural processes on reanalysis data to model global aerosol absorption optical depth from sparse AERONET station observations, then uses the probabilistic predictions in an active learning loop to propose new sensor locations that maximally reduce uncertainty.
